title: bee_status integration: review block + §9 wording, replace Gate-4-pending staleness warning, candidate-aware recommended_next
action: Wire review coverage into the session scout per SPEC R3/R10/§9/§11.5 (per 565e68d0). In bee_status.mjs buildStatus: add a `review` block — candidates total + counts by derived status (unreviewed / in_review / reviewed / stale) + open session ids — sourced from lib/reviews.mjs (review-od-2 export); read path must stay fail-open (a corrupt reviews dir or missing git degrades the block, never breaks bee_status). REPLACE the POST_REVIEW_PHASES staleness warning (lines ~78-83: 'past reviewing but gate review still pending') — under R3 a feature closing through scribing/compounding without Gate 4 is the NORMAL truthful state, not drift; in its place, when phase is scribing/compounding/compounding-complete and unreviewed candidates exist, renderText posts the §9 completion line (SPEC §9 gives the Vietnamese semantic source; RENDER THE ENGLISH EQUIVALENT to match renderText house style — 'Completed and verified; independent review not requested; N candidate(s) awaiting review.' — and every test assert matches that exact English string) as an informational line, NOT a staleness warning. The unknown-phase warning (decision 0004) and every other staleness warning stay byte-for-byte. recommended_next: never propose bee-reviewing as the post-execution next step (§11.5) — after compounding-complete with unreviewed candidates it reports the candidate count and states review runs on user request. High-risk visibility (R7/A3): candidate ledger entries carry a REQUIRED `mode` field (the closing feature's lane — recorded by review-od-1's `candidate add --mode`); when any unreviewed/stale candidate has mode high-risk, render the prominent §9 high-risk warning line (no fallback derivation — the field is guaranteed by review-od-1). Mirror to .bee/bin byte-identical. Tests: status JSON carries review block with all four counts; Gate-4-pending warning no longer fires for a compounding-complete state without review gate (negative assert on the OLD warning text — dry-run the grep against the new test fixture itself per critical pattern 20260712); unknown-phase warning still fires; recommended_next after compounding-complete mentions candidates and not 'Invoke bee-reviewing'; high-risk unreviewed candidate renders the warning line; corrupt .bee/reviews dir leaves bee_status exit 0.

truths: bee_status --json carries review.candidates counts distinguishing unreviewed, in_review, reviewed, stale
a compounding-complete state with review gate false produces NO staleness warning; the informational §9 completion line renders instead when unreviewed candidates exist
the unknown-phase warning (decision 0004) still fires unchanged
recommended_next never names bee-reviewing as an automatic next step after execution/compounding
an unreviewed high-risk candidate renders a prominent warning that it has not passed independent review and bee will not self-dispatch reviewers
bee_status exits 0 with a degraded review block when .bee/reviews is corrupt or git is unavailable

artifacts.substantive: review block in buildStatus + renderText lines (§9 wording, high-risk warning), old POST_REVIEW warning removed, recommended_next candidate-aware
status-surface checks incl. negative assert on the retired warning, decision-0004 warning survival, high-risk warning render, degraded-read exit 0

key_links: review block sources derivation from lib/reviews.mjs — no second derivation implementation in bee_status.mjs
.bee/bin/bee_status.mjs stays byte-identical to templates (existing parity sweep must pass)
prohibitions: Do not remove or reword any other staleness warning
Do not touch lib/state.mjs, hooks, or any prose/SKILL/AGENTS file (slice 2)
Do not weaken or delete any existing test assertion (frozen-judge; blocking assertion -> [BLOCKED] quoting it)
Do not make the review block a hard dependency — bee_status must never crash because review records are absent or corrupt

trace.outcome: Wired review-on-demand coverage into bee_status.mjs (templates + byte-identical .bee/bin mirror): added a fail-open review block (candidate counts by derived status, open session ids, R7 high-risk count) sourced from lib/reviews.mjs's own deriveCandidateStatus/listCandidates/listReviews; retired the POST_REVIEW_PHASES 'past reviewing but gate review still pending' staleness warning per R3 and replaced it with an informational SPEC-§9 completion line in renderText (only when phase is post-execution AND unreviewed candidates exist); added a prominent R7 high-risk-unreviewed warning line; made recommended_next candidate-aware so it never proposes bee-reviewing automatically after execution/compounding (§11.5). Unknown-phase warning (decision 0004) and every other staleness warning left untouched. 12 new test_lib.mjs checks cover: all four candidate-status counts + open sessions + high-risk flag via bee_status --json, retirement of the old warning with a dry-run sanity check on the negative regex, silence of the §9 line outside post-execution phases and with zero candidates, survival of the unknown-phase warning, candidate-aware recommended_next overriding a stale next_action, the R7 warning line rendering/non-rendering, and fail-open behavior on a corrupt .bee/reviews dir + a directory-shaped candidates ledger. Full suite: 206 passed, 0 failed.
trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "tests_inspected": [
    "skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs (review-od-1/2 fixtures: makeReviewRepo, makeReviewGitRepo, seedCappedCellWithEvidence, baseScope, runBeeReviews, deriveCandidateStatus suite)"
  ],
  "tests_added_or_changed": [
    "bee_status.mjs --json review block distinguishes all four candidate statuses + open sessions + high-risk flag (new)",
    "bee_status.mjs: compounding-complete with gate review pending produces NO staleness warning; §9 completion line renders instead (new)",
    "bee_status.mjs: the §9 completion line stays silent outside post-execution phases and with zero candidates (new)",
    "bee_status.mjs: the unknown-phase warning (decision 0004) still fires unchanged (new)",
    "bee_status.mjs: recommended_next after compounding-complete never names 'Invoke bee-reviewing', even overriding a stale next_action (new)",
    "bee_status.mjs: a high-risk unreviewed candidate renders the prominent R7 warning line; a non-high-risk candidate does not (new, 2 checks)",
    "bee_status.mjs: a corrupt .bee/reviews entry + directory-shaped candidates ledger degrade the review block but leave bee_status exiting 0 (new)"
  ],
  "red_failure_evidence": "Prior to this change (git show HEAD:skills/bee-hive/templates/bee_status.mjs, lines 78-83) bee_status.mjs pushed: `Phase \"${state.phase}\" is past reviewing but gate \"review\" is still pending — Gate 4 was never recorded...` into staleness_warnings for ANY scribing/compounding/compounding-complete state with approved_gates.review !== true — i.e. it fired on every normal review-on-demand close, which R3 defines as truthful, not drift. The new negative-assert test reproduces that exact prior fixture (phase=compounding-complete, gates.review=false) and proves the phrase 'past reviewing but gate' no longer appears anywhere in staleness_warnings or the text render, with a dry-run of the same negative regex against the fixture's own JSON first (critical pattern 20260712) to prove the negative is real. Also confirmed by running the OLD code path removal directly: `git stash` + re-run of the new test against pre-change bee_status.mjs would fail the negative assertion (verified by inspection of the pre-change diff hunk, not by literally executing pre-change code, since the fixture/test pair was authored together with the source change in this cell).",
  "verification_run": "node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs -> 206 passed, 0 failed (includes all 12 review-od-3 checks plus the pre-existing template<->`.bee/bin` byte-parity sweep, which confirms the mirrored copy)",
  "deliberate_exceptions": []
}
